localBiteReport: Note: The most current on-the-water reports found are approximately 6 days to 2 weeks old (early-to-mid July 2026), not same-week daily updates. That said, here's what local guides are reporting: speckled trout have been the standout inshore species, with a strong bite described as plentiful and active across the bay system, including out of Santa Rosa Beach. Redfish are also showing well — one Santa Rosa Beach guide recently documented a 40-inch, 26-lb bull red caught in the bay. One Gulf Breeze-area guide noted the inshore bite had been tough but was described as improving daily as of about a week ago, with weather and wind being the main limiting factor rather than fish presence. Water temps are sitting in the low 80s, consistent with mid-summer conditions that typically favor early morning trout on grass flats and reds pushing into shallower backwater areas on moving tides.
localBiteSource: CaptainExperiences.com (Choctawhatchee Bay and Destin report pages, reports dated ~6 days to 2 weeks ago), CaptainExperiences.com Destin report (Gulf Breeze guide, ~1 week ago) · Auto-refreshed via Claude API
